lenses.guidance: v 1 announces 'one more plague' — the chapter functions as a pause before climax, framing ex 12 in advance. The narrative repetition of 'against Pharaoh, against Egypt' (vv 4-7) marks the structural seam between cycle and Passover.

The firstborn theme inaugurated here (v 5) threads across Scripture — Cain/Abel, Esau/Jacob, Manasseh/Ephraim, finally Christ as 'firstborn of all creation' (Col 1:15) and 'firstborn from the dead' (Rev 1:5).
Moses 'went out from Pharaoh in hot anger' (v 8). Faith permits — sometimes requires — anger at hardened cruelty. Pray your indignation honestly, but channel it into obedience as Moses does in ex 12.
